intro: The clearest split is that Braze carries high lock-in and Airship carries low lock-in. Braze is Customer engagement platform with push, in-app messages, email, SMS, WhatsApp, and Canvas journey orchestration; Airship is Mobile engagement platform with push notifications, in-app messaging, SMS, email, and real-time data streaming. The data points Braze at mobile app engagement and user lifecycle messaging, and Airship at mobile app engagement and user lifecycle messaging. Pricing: Braze is subscription from $10; Airship is subscription from $9. Lock-in is high for Braze and low for Airship, with DX at 5/5 vs 4/5 and transparency at 1/5 vs 2/5. On listed strengths, Braze emphasises mobile app engagement and user lifecycle messaging, and Airship emphasises mobile app engagement and user lifecycle messaging. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits web-only products without mobile, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Braze is for mobile; Airship is for mobile; decide based on how much lock-in you tolerate.
verdict_a: Choose Braze if deeper lock-in is acceptable for the feature depth and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is web-only products without mobile.
verdict_b: Choose Airship if you prefer low lock-in and easier exit and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is web-only products without mobile.

faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $10 for Braze and $9 for Airship. Neither lists a free tier. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Braze to Airship?
faq.a: Braze is tagged with high lock-in (engagement platform, deep user data). Airship is tagged with low lock-in (audiences and channels bound to airship data model). At least one side is high lock-in, so budget migration time for re-implementing integrations.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Braze or Airship?
faq.a: The dataset rates Braze at 5/5 for developer experience and Airship at 4/5, so Braze scores higher. That score reflects overall polish rather than any single integration, so trial both against your stack before committing.

faq.q: Is Airship a good alternative to Braze?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches mobile, since both list the same fit. Airship is flagged as not for web-only products without mobile, so confirm your context does not fall there.
